skill.name: domain-modeling
skill.description: Build and maintain a project domain model by defining terms, resolving language conflicts, and recording durable architectural decisions.
skill.summary: Create shared domain language and focused ADRs for software projects.
skill.author: mattpocock

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found these are false positives in Markdown examples, file names, and documentation text. No executable scripts, network calls, environment access, prompt injection attempts, or malicious behavior were found in the audited files.

low_findings.title: False Positive External Command Matches
low_findings.description: Backtick-delimited Markdown examples and file paths were flagged as Ruby or shell execution. The reviewed locations are documentation text, not executable code, and contain no command invocation logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matched text is Markdown prose or fenced examples. There are no scripts, interpreters, shell calls, or dynamic execution paths in these files.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts are triggered by Markdown file references, headings, and explanatory text. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, host inspection, system probing, or data collection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found only documentation instructions and examples. The files do not contain crypto APIs, hashing calls, OS commands, or reconnaissance workflows.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when business terms, boundaries, and durable decisions are unclear. This skill helps Claude, Codex, or Claude Code build a concise glossary and record only meaningful architectural decisions.
actual_capabilities: Identifies unclear, overloaded, or conflicting domain terms during a design session.
Maintains a focused CONTEXT.md glossary for project-specific business language.
Distinguishes single-context repositories from multi-context repositories with CONTEXT-MAP.md.
Suggests ADRs only for hard-to-reverse, surprising, trade-off-based decisions.
Provides compact formats for domain context files and ADR documents.
Cross-checks stated domain behavior against repository code when useful.
limitations: It does not create a full product specification or implementation plan.
It depends on user confirmation when domain terms are ambiguous.
It records implementation decisions only as ADRs, not inside CONTEXT.md.
It does not replace expert domain knowledge from stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Clarify Domain Terms
use_cases.description: Resolve overloaded words and choose canonical names before the team encodes them in APIs, database tables, or documentation.

use_cases.title: Maintain Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Capture short ADRs for choices that are costly to reverse and likely to confuse future maintainers.

use_cases.title: Map Bounded Contexts
use_cases.description: Separate terms and relationships across multiple contexts so each area has a clear language and ownership boundary.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Domain Glossary
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: create the first context file for a repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the domain-modeling skill to help me create a CONTEXT.md glossary for this project. Ask about unclear business terms before writing definitions.

prompt_templates.title: Resolve Conflicting Terms
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: reconcile language used by users, code, and documentation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the current domain language and identify terms that conflict or overlap. Propose canonical terms and update the glossary after I confirm them.

prompt_templates.title: Decide Whether an ADR Is Needed
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: evaluate an architectural choice before documenting it.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ADR criteria from the domain-modeling skill. Tell me whether this decision deserves an ADR, then draft the shortest useful record.

prompt_templates.title: Model Multiple Contexts
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced: organize language across several bounded contexts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository for multiple contexts. Build or update a CONTEXT-MAP.md and keep each context glossary focused on its own domain language.

output_examples.input: We use customer, account, and user interchangeably in this checkout flow.
output_examples.output: The skill would ask which concept owns authentication, which one places orders, and which one receives invoices. It would then propose canonical terms and avoided alternatives.

output_examples.input: We chose event sourcing for orders because partial cancellation and audit history matter.
output_examples.output: The skill would check whether the choice is hard to reverse, surprising, and trade-off based. If confirmed, it would draft a concise ADR explaining the decision.

output_examples.input: Billing and fulfillment both reference orders but use different meanings for shipment.
output_examples.output: The skill would separate the contexts, define each meaning locally, and describe the relationship between contexts in a context map.

best_practices: Confirm each domain term with stakeholders before recording it as canonical.
Keep CONTEXT.md limited to domain language, not implementation details.
Create ADRs only when a future maintainer needs the decision context.
anti_patterns: Using the glossary as a general project specification or scratch pad.
Recording every technical choice as an ADR without a real trade-off.
Adding broad programming terms that are not specific to the domain.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or updates CONTEXT.md, CONTEXT-MAP.md, and short ADR files when the project needs them.

faq.question: Does it write implementation code?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on domain language, context boundaries, and decision documentation.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it when terms are unclear, contexts overlap, or an architectural decision needs a short durable record.

faq.question: Can it support multiple bounded contexts?
faq.answer: Yes. It uses CONTEXT-MAP.md to identify contexts and points each context to its own glossary.

faq.question: Will it create ADRs for every decision?
faq.answer: No. It creates ADRs only for decisions that are hard to reverse, surprising, and based on a real trade-off.

faq.question: Is it safe for marketplace use?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed files are Markdown documentation and contain no executable code, network access, or prompt injection attempts.
